acquisition method: The Orbitrap was operated in 'Data Dependent Acquisition' mode
alkylation reagent: 20 mM iodoacetamide, which was incubated for 30 min in the dark.
alkylation concentration: 20 mM iodoacetamide, which was incubated for 30 min in the dark.
cleavage agent: 50 ng of LysC was added to the beads and incubated overnight at 37 °C.
fragmentation method: with Orbitrap accumulation at R = 50,000 of HCD fragmented parent ions.
instrument: elution via a 50 cm × 75 μm EasySpray C18 column into a Lumos Tribrid Orbitrap mass spectrometer.
ionization type: A 70′ gradient of 6–40% B was used, followed by washing and re-equilibration (A = 2% MeCN, 0.1% formic acid; B = 80% MeCN, 0.1% formic acid).
labeling: Each set of conditions were then labeled using eight channels of a TMT10plex.
reduction reagent: 10 mM DTT in 50 mM HEPES pH 8.0 for 15 min at RT.
reduction concentration: 10 mM DTT in 50 mM HEPES pH 8.0 for 15 min at RT.
